US Stocks Surge Following 90-Day US-China Trade Truce, Dow Jumps Nearly 1,000 Points
body: Stocks soar after the US and China agree to a 90-day truce in their trade war, with Dow up 957 points and S&P 500 climbing 2.6%.

title: Panasonic Layoffs Don't Affect Indonesia's Operations, Industry Ministry Says
body: Panasonic's global job cuts won't affect its Indonesian operations, with the country remaining a key production hub in Southeast Asia.

title: Indonesia Plans to Restrict Import of Cassava, Tapioca to Protect Local Farmers
body: Indonesia plans import restrictions on cassava and tapioca to protect local farmers, amid rising imports, primarily from Vietnam.

title: Indonesia Ranks Top 3 in ASEAN Competitiveness, So Why Are Investors Still Hesitant?
body: Indonesia hits record 27th in global competitiveness, but high costs, red tape, and local intimidation continue to drive investors away.

title: US Agrees to Drop Its 145% Tariff Rate on China to 30%
body: China agreed to lower its rate on US goods by the same amount to 10 percent.

title: Indonesia Says Local Content Reforms Are Domestic-Driven, Not Response to US Tariffs
body: The USTR has cited Indonesia’s local content regulations as a trade barrier that prompted the reciprocal tariffs.

title: US Makes 'Substantial Progress' in China Tariff Talks, Scott Bessent Says
body: There was no immediate comment from the Chinese representatives at the talks.

title: Pertamina to Phase Out Fuel Imports from Singapore Following Government Order
body: Singapore currently accounts for around 60 percent of Indonesia’s total fuel imports.

title: No Breakthroughs Yet in US-China Tariff Talks, But Trump Touts 'Great Progress'
body: In its editorial, Xinhua said, “Talks should never be a pretext for continued coercion or extortion."

title: Indonesia Reviews Reinsurance Rules Amid Growing Deficit
body: Indonesia recorded a Rp 12.1 trillion deficit in reinsurance transactions last year, marking a continued increase in recent years.

title: Indonesia to Cultivate 300,000 Hectares of Sugar Palm to Power Bioethanol Push
body: “With proper cultivation, one hectare of sugar palm can produce 24,000 liters of bioethanol," Raja Juli Antoni said.

title: Chinese Car Sales Soar 153% in Indonesia as Japanese Brands Lose Ground
body: Chinese brands now command 10 percent of the Indonesian auto market, a sharp increase from just 3.83 percent a year earlier.

title: Expert: Trump Wrong to Blame Asia for US Job Losses
body: Trump ignores the fact that the majority of profits for US companies now come from services, not factories.

title: VinFast to Invest $300M for 63,000 EV Charging Stations in Indonesia
body: VinFast and V-Green will invest $300M to build 63,000 EV charging stations across Indonesia by 2025.

title: Indonesia to Slash Fuel Imports from Singapore, Eyes Middle East Supply
body: Indonesia plans to cut fuel imports from Singapore due to high costs, shifting toward cheaper Middle Eastern suppliers.

title: Airlangga Invites Japanese Chamber Members to Invest in Indonesia’s Strategic Projects
body: Japan currently ranks as Indonesia’s sixth-largest source of foreign investment, with over 12,800 projects to date.

title: Indonesia Ends Three-Month Decline in Consumer Confidence
body: Indonesian consumers are growing more confident of the economy, the central bank says.

title: Indonesia Among the Least Protective Economies, Industry Ministry Says
body: Indonesia currently imposes only around 370 NTBs. In contrast, China has more than 2,800 such policies and India over 2,500.

title: Lawmaker Warns Minister Maruarar Against Converting Jakarta Prison into Housing
body: Maruarar has said the inmates at Cipinang could be relocated to a new facility outside urban areas.

title: State Budget Faces Major Shortfall as SOE Dividends Shift to Danantara
body: The most recent dividend payment received by the state budget was Rp 10.8 trillion ($665 million), transferred by BRI in January.

title: Trade Ministry Launches “Local Thursday” Movement to Promote Indonesian Products
body: Budi said the campaign will not be limited to Thursdays and hopes it will inspire broader adoption throughout the week.

title: Rupiah Weakens, Foreign Reserves Dip to $152.5 Billion in April
body: Indonesia’s forex reserves fell to $152.5 billion in April, down $4.6 billion, as BI intervenes to stabilize the rupiah.

title: The More Trump Talks About Making Trade Deals, The More Confusing The Tariff Picture Gets
body: His team seems good with that, saying Trump is using “strategic uncertainty” to his advantage.

title: Antam Stock Poised to Break Rp 3,000 Mark amid Bullish Gold Market
body: The upward momentum comes amid a historic rally in global gold prices, which reached $3,400 per ounce in April.

title: Danantara Trust Fund Wants to Be ASEAN’s Philanthropic Hub
body: Danantara says that its yet-to-be-established philanthropic arm will be transparent and follow international standards.

title: Danantara to Set Up Philanthropic Arm, Hopes to Partner with Bill Gates
body: Danantara plans to allocate up to 2.5 percent of its divident payout ratio to this philantropic arm.

title: Garuda Group Grounds 15 Aircraft Due to Spare Parts Supply Issues
body: Disruptions in the spare parts supply chain have led to extended maintenance times, as the necessary components are difficult to procure.

title: Seven EV Makers Eye Rp 15 Trillion Investment in Indonesian Plants
body: Indonesia's market share of electric cars rose to 5 percent of total vehicle sales in 2024, up from just 1.7 percent in 2023.

title: Top US Officials Will Meet with Chinese Delegation in Switzerland in First Major Talks of Trade War
body: A government spokesperson said China would not "sacrifice its principles or global equity or justice in seeking any agreement.”

title: Toyota Acquires 40 Pct of Astra's Used Car Company for $120 Million
body: Toyota invests $120M for a 40% stake in Astra’s OLX unit, targeting Indonesia’s used-car market through digital expansion.

title: Trump Tariff War: Norway Calls on Indonesia to Maintain Global Trade System
body: Norway, a country that will get hit by Trump's 15 percent import tax, wants Indonesia to help maintain global trade system.

title: Prabowo Wants State-Run Banks to Have Fewer Commissioners
body: The government-run banks such as BNI and Mandiri have a six-member board of commissioners.

title: Indonesia is Banking on Welfare Programs After Disappointing Q1 GDP Results, Airlangga Says
body: Indonesia boosts social aid and expands free meals to 82.9M people, aiming to lift growth after Q1 GDP fell short at 4.87%.

title: VKTR Secures Order for 80 CKD Electric Buses from TransJakarta
body: This latest order will bring VKTR's total operational electric bus fleet to 152 units.

title: Warning Signs Flash as Indonesia's Factory Sector Contracts Sharply
body: Indonesia’s April PMI fell to 46.7, the lowest since Aug 2021, as global tariffs, layoffs, and weak demand squeeze the manufacturing sector.

title: Bappenas Reveals Prabowo’s 6.3 Percent Growth Target for 2026
body: The government is also aiming to eradicate extreme poverty and reduce the overall poverty rate to between 6.5 percent and 7.5 percent.

title: BPS: 7.28 Million Indonesians Jobless, with Gen Z Most Affected
body: Indonesia’s jobless count hits 7.28 million in February 2025, with Gen Z facing the highest unemployment rate among all age groups.

title: Japan-Backed Muara Laboh Geothermal Reaches Financial Close for Expansion
body: Muara Laboh geothermal project's second unit will enter operations in 2027.

title: Over 24,000 Workers Laid Off in Indonesia So Far This Year
body: The largest number of layoffs in the past five years occurred in 2020 at the onset of the COVID-19 pandemic, affecting 386,877 workers.

title: Indonesia’s Q1 Economic Growth Slows to 4.87 Pct
body: This was the lowest Q1 growth that Indonesia had ever recorded over the past three years.
